title: quercus macrocarpa subsp. oliviformis
intro: Quercus macrocarpa subsp. oliviformis, also known as the bur oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree in the beech family. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Quercus macrocarpa subsp. oliviformis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
